Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PpADT-C as APA32584.1.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper analyzes ADT and PDT activities of PpADT-C, i.e. a bifunctional arogenate/prephenate dehydratase.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results and enzyme assays explicitly measure ADT activity producing Phe from arogenate and PDT activity producing phenylpyruvate from prephenate.

Primary_Substrate.reason: ADT assays used arogenate (Agn) and PDT assays used prephenate/Ppa.

Primary_Product.reason: ADT product quantified was phenylalanine; PDT assay quantified phenylpyruvate after prephenate conversion.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The study concerns ADTs in phenylalanine biosynthesis via the arogenate route in plants.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ADT is presented as catalyzing the phenylalanine-forming end step of the pathway.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: ADT/PDT is treated as the terminal step leading to Phe formation.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: ADT activity directly forms phenylalanine, the metabolite measured in planta.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper strongly argues ADT is feedback regulated and physiologically important, but does not explicitly designate PpADT-C itself as a rate-limiting step.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Results explicitly state maritime pine, Pinus pinaster.

Applied_Species.reason: Transient overexpression experiments were performed in Nicotiana benthamiana plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Overexpression section and Results specify N. benthamiana.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state plant expression constructs used pGWB11 with CaMV P35S promoter.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The final metabolite measured in planta was phenylalanine.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion explicitly describes Phe as required for protein biosynthesis and as precursor of phenylpropanoids/lignin-related compounds.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show PpADT-C is weakly inhibited by Phe and its overexpression caused ~145-fold Phe accumulation versus GFP.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Phe accumulation was measured in leaves of infiltrated plants.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Methods and Results describe transient overexpression in leaves.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify Agrobacterium infiltration and collection 72 h after infiltration.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Methods state the putative plastid transit peptide was removed for recombinant expression, implying plastid targeting, but direct localization of PpADT-C was not experimentally shown here.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper describes ADTs as ACT-domain enzymes and classifies PpADT-C as type-II lacking the PAC sequence.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These exact approaches are reported: recombinant ADT/PDT assays, IC50 analyses, transient overexpression in N. benthamiana, UPLC/MS Phe determination, and western blotting.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Conclusion explicitly states identified sequence motifs provide a biotechnological target to engineer AAA and derived-compound production; PpADT-C exemplifies deregulated ADTs.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results: type-II relaxed inhibition, no substantial ADT inhibition up to 100 μM Phe with PDT IC50 ~320 μM, and ~145-fold Phe accumulation in planta.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results identify sequence changes in the ACT/PAC region associated with deregulation and use PpADT-C as the main type-II model.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PpADT-G as APA32588.1.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper assays both ADT and PDT activities for PpADT-G, consistent with arogenate/prephenate dehydratase.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results directly assay conversion of arogenate to Phe and prephenate to phenylpyruvate.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Arogenate and prephenate/Ppa were the substrates used.

Primary_Product.reason: ADT product measured was Phe and PDT product measured was phenylpyruvate.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PpADT-G is studied within phenylalanine biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ADT catalyzes the Phe-forming terminal branch step.

Applied_Species.reason: Overexpression was done in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion states Phe is a proteinogenic amino acid and precursor for phenylpropanoids/lignin-related metabolites.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show strong Phe feedback inhibition and only ~4-fold Phe increase upon overexpression.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Transient overexpression in leaves is described.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify Agrobacterium infiltration and 72 h sampling.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Presence of a putative plastid transit peptide is implied by removal for bacterial expression, but localization was not directly tested in this paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PpADT-G is described as an ADT/PDT family enzyme with ACT domain and type-I PAC sequence.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results include recombinant assays, kinetic Phe inhibition analyses, PAC swapping, and transient overexpression with metabolite profiling.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Paper presents regulated versus deregulated alleles to identify motifs for engineering; this comparison value is directly aligned with conclusions.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match Results/Table 1/Figure 4: type-I strong inhibition, ADT IC50 27.6 μM and PDT IC50 47.7 μM, and ~4-fold Phe increase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper implies PpADT-A is a type-II enzyme in Figure 4 context and Discussion mentions it as type-II in compression wood, but this classification is not directly demonstrated in the Results text here.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana and UPLC/MS metabolite profiling were performed.
